name: Battlefield of Austerlitz
description: Widely regarded as the eatest victory achieved by Napoleon, the Battle of Austerlitz (also known as the Battle of the Three Emperors) destroyed the Third Coalition.

name: Battlefield of Waterloo
description: The Battle of Waterloo on June 18, 1815 was Napoleon's final defeat. The Duke of Wellington and Prussian Marshal Blücher's combined forces ended Napoleon's rule as Emperor of France. The Lion's Mound memorial overlooks the battlefield.

name: Arc de Triomphe
description: Commissioned by Napoleon after his victory at Austerlitz, the monument honors those who fought and died for France in the French Revolutionary and Napoleonic Wars.

name: Les Invalides (Napoleon's Tomb)
desciption: The Dôme des Invalides houses the monumental tomb of Napoleon Bonaparte. His remains were returned from Saint Helena in 1840 and placed in a grand sarcophagus of red quartzite. The site is one of the most visited monuments in Paris.

name: Battlefield of Marengo
description: A battle fought between French forces under the First Consul Napoleon Bonaparte and Austrian forces near the city of Alessandria, in Piedmont, Italy.

name: Château de Fontainebleau
description: One of Napoleon's favorite residences, Fontainebleau witnessed his famous farewell to the Old Guard in 1814. Napoleon signed his first abdication here and bid farewell in the Cour des Adieux (Courtyard of Farewells).

name: Battle of Borodino / Moskova
description: The Battle of Borodino on September 7, 1812 was the bloodiest single-day battle in the Napoleonic Wars. Though technically a French victory, the massive losses weakened Napoleon's Grande Armée and foreshadowed the disastrous retreat from Moscow.

name: Casa Buonaparte
description: The ancestral home of the Bonaparte family and the birthplace of Napoleon Bonaparte.

name: Estampe Combat de Saint Vicente
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme.

name: Estampe Bataille de Tudela
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme.

name: Estampe Siège et prise de Valence
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme.

name: Battlefield of Raszyn
description: The first Battle of Raszyn was fought on 19 April 1809 between armies of the Austrian Empire under Archduke Ferdinand Karl Joseph of Austria-Este and the Duchy of Warsaw under Józef Antoni Poniatowski, as part of the War of the Fifth Coalition in the Napoleonic Wars. The battle was not decisive, but it did result in the Austrians obtaining their goal by capturing the Polish capital Warsaw.

name: Death of Godebski at Raszyn
description: The artist, pupil of Horace Vernet, depicts  the death of the Polish writer and soldier Cyprian Godebski while Józef Poniatowski rides on horseback in the centre of the scene.

name: Battle of Raszyn
description: In this monumental composition, the artist depicts the first moments of the Battle of Raszyn.

name: Battle of Somosierra
description: Suchodolski specialised in battle scenes. This painting is a representative example of his later work. In the centre of his compositions, he laces the most famous of the battle’s participants, in this case Colonel Kozietulski, on a white horse.

name: Battlefield of Mount Tabor
description: The Battle of Mount Tabor was fought on 16 April 1799 between French forces commanded by Napoleon Bonaparte and General Jean-Baptiste Kléber, against an Ottoman Army under Abdullah Pasha al-Azm, ruler of Damascus.

name: Bonaparte's camp
description: Bonaparte's encampment before the Battle of Mount Tabor.

name: Medjameh bridge over the Jordan
description: Bridge over the Jordan, through which some of the Ottomans fled after being defeated at the Battle of Mount Tabor.

name: Battle of Bailén - Spanish left wing
description: A milestone indicates the position where the Spanish west wing was deployed ocuppying the hills of Cerrajón and Haza Walona.

name: Battle of Bailén - bridge over the Rumblar
description: The day of the battle, at dawn, the French column reached the bridge over the Rumblar river which they crossed pushing back a Spanish detachment.

name: Battle of Bailén - Haza Walona hill
description: This hill was where the Swiss regiments of both sides clashed. Upon recognizing each other, and because their contracts prevented them from engaging other Swiss regiments, those enlisted in the French army joined the Spanish.

name: Battle of Bailén - Cerrajón hill
description: The French right wing extended up to this hill at the initial moves of the battle.

name: Battle of Bailén - Post house
description: Negotiations after the battle took place here. The capitulation was finally signed on the 22nd, after many discussions.

name: Battle of Alcolea
description: The Battle of Alcolea Bridge was a minor battle that took place on June 7th 1808, during the Peninsular War, at Alcolea, a small village 10 km from Córdoba, the city that would be invaded by French troops later that same afternoon

name: Pablo Muñoz house site
description: A hero of the War of Independence (Peninsula War), he settled at this site after retiring from the Army in 1845.

name: Battle of Alcolea - Bridge
description: Its construction began in 1787 during the reign of Charles III. During the battle, the grenadier company of the Paris Guard Battalion attacked the Spanish entrenched at the entrance to the bridge, managing to overpower them and penetrate the enclosure, which led the Spanish to retreat. At the same time, the bulk of the French forces seized the bridge and began to cross it.

name: Battle of Bailén - La Cruz Blanca
description: The French troops advanced up to this point where they were driven back by the Spanish vanguard.

name: Battle of Bailén - Nuria del Sordo
description: The battle took place on a very hot summer day and the dwell at the farm existed at that date was a key point for the French to capture.

name: Episodio de la batalla de Bailén
location: Biblioteca Histórica Marqués de Valdecilla, Madrid
description: Not on display. Artist Ricardo Balaca y Orejas Canseco

name: Museo de la batalla de Bailén
Monumento a la victoria de Bailén
description: Monument created in 1987 by the architect Vicente Sánchez and the sculptor Antonio Rivas.

name: French fortification at Somosierra
description: Site of a French fortification built in 1808 as ordered Napoleon after the Battle of Somosierra.

name: Puente de Somosierra
description: Site of a French fortification built in 1808 as ordered Napoleon after the Battle of Somosierra.

name: Batalla de Uclés (Uclez)
description: Derrota española ante el mariscal Victor.

name: Acción de la Fuensanta
description: La acción de la Fuensanta fue un combate llevado a cabo el 12 de noviembre de 1810 entre las tropas de Pedro Villacampa y las del general Józef Chlopicki en el Santuario de la Fuensanta de Villel.

name: Acción de Orgaz
description: Enfrentó a una columna del Tercer Ejército español compuesta por unos ciento veinte jinetes y doscientos infantes, al mando del coronel Marcelino Villa, con una fuerza francesa de unos ochocientos jinetes pertenecientes a unidades de húsares y cazadores a caballo.

name: Campo de Batalla de Alcañiz
description: El 23 de mayo de 1809 tuvo lugar la batalla de Alcañiz en la que las tropas españolas al mando de Joaquín Blake vencieron a las francesas comandadas por Louis Gabriel Suchet.

name: Batalla de Alcañiz - Cerro la Perdiguera
description: En el cerro Perdiguera se sitúa el extremo del flanco izquierdo del ejército español con las tropas al mando del general don Pedro Roca con 2000 hombres de ellos 1400 soldados de infantería que defienden la posición y tres cañones con sus artilleros.

name: Batalla de Alcañiz - Cerro del Pueyo
description: En este cerro,ante la ermita de la Virgen de los Pueyos bloqueando la entrada a Alcañiz, se sitúa el ala derecha española con una brigada de 2000 hombres y un cañón al mando del brigadier don Carlos de Areizaga.

name: Batalla de Alba de Tormes 1809
description: El 28 de noviembre de 1809, un cuerpo imperial francés al mando de François Étienne de Kellermann atacó a un ejército español liderado por Diego de Cañas y Portocarrero, duque del Parque.

name: Castillo de Alba de Tormes
description: Exposición que se encuentra en Alba de Tormes en el torreón de los Duques de Alba, en la  que se habla de la Batalla librada el 28 de noviembre de 1809, en la que General Gabriel de Mendizábal Iraeta formó con las tropas bajo su mando varios cuadros de Infantería contra los que se estrellarán hasta tres cargas de la Caballería francesa al mando de Kellermann.

name: Batalla de Alba de Tormes 1812
description: La batalla de Alba de Tormes fue una batalla librada entre los días 8 y 16 de noviembre de 1812 entre las fuerzas francesas al mando del Mariscal Jean-de-Dieu Soult y las fuerzas aliadas (británicas, portuguesas y españolas) al mando del Duque de Wellington.

name: Batalla de los Arapiles (Salamanca)
description: Librada el 22 de julio de 1812 en los alrededores de las colinas conocidas como Arapil Chico y Arapil Grande y fue una gran victoria del ejército anglo-luso-español al mando del general Arthur Wellesley, primer duque de Wellington, contra las tropas francesas al mando del mariscal Marmont.

name: Ermita nuestra Señora de la peña
description: En los alrrededores de esta ermita comenzaron las escaramuzas entre las vanguardias de ambos ejércitos el día de la batalla.

name: Primer Sitio de Astorga
description: El primer sitio tuvo lugar entre el 21 de marzo y el 22 de abril de 1810 y como resultado del mismo se produjo la conquista francesa de la ciudad, que la retuvo en sus manos durante más de dos años.

name: Brecha en la muralla
description: Zona de la murall por donde los franceses abrieron brecha en 1810.

name: Fuente encalada
description: Esta fuente fue tomada por los franceces el 1 de abril de 1810, cortando el suministro de agua a la ciudad. Luego, en este sitio,el 22 de abril, la guarnición entregó sus armas tras rendir la plaza.

name: Monumento de los Sitios
description: Monumento en honor a los defensores de la ciudad durante los sitios de Astorga. Obra del escultor Enrique Marín Higuero. Se erigió en 1910, con motivo del centenario del primer sitio de Astorga

name: Batalla de Ordal
description: Librada entre los días 12 y 13 de septiembre de 1813 entre las fuerzas francesas del mariscal Louis Suchet y las anglo-españolas mandadas por William Bentinck. Fue la última victoria del ejército francés en la península ibérica.

name: Puente del Lledoner
description: Puente de 32 metros de altura y 192 de largo mandado construir en 1761 por orden del Rey Carlos III y finalizado en 1803. Las tropas del Mariscal Suchet cruzaron este puente la noche previa a la batalla de Ordal.

name: Das Dorfmuseum Großgörschen
description: This local museum includes a diorama of the Battle of Großgörschen of May 2, 1813.

name: Puente de Cruzul
description: Puente cruzado por las tropas del General Moore en 1809 durante su retirada hacia A Coruña.

name: Maqueta batalla Puente de Cruzul
description: Maqueta de la batalla de Puente de Cruzul en la Casa Consistorial de Becerreá.

name: Batalla de Usagre
description: La batalla de Usagre se desarrolló el 25 de mayo de 1811 en Usagre, España, y enfrentó la caballería francesa del general de Latour-Maubourg a la caballería anglo-hispano-portuguesa del mayor general William Lumley. El enfrentamiento terminó con una victoria de los aliados hispano-anglo-portugueses.

name: Obelisco Héroes de la Independencia de la Batalla de Villagodio
description: Obelisco conmemorativo de la batalla de Villagodio. «Los zamoranos de 1908 dedican esta inscripción a los héroes del 6 de enero de 1809. Este monumento fue erigido en 1819

name: Batalla de Bidasoa (o Larrún)
description: La batalla de Bidasoa, también conocida como batalla de Larrún, fue una batalla librada el 7 de octubre de 1813 entre el ejército francés comandado por el mariscal Soult y el ejército aliado anglo-hispano-portugués comandado por el duque de Wellington. La batalla terminó con una victoria aliada.

name: Pico de Larrún
description: El 7 de octubre de 1813 La División Ligera de James Kempt y la española de Francisco de Longa atacaron a los franceses que ocupaban la cumbre del Larrún.

name: Batalla del Puente de Triana
description: La batalla del Puente de Triana tuvo lugar el 27 de agosto de 1812 para poder tomar la ciudad de Sevilla.

name: Real Alcázar de Sevilla
description: El Alcázar fue utilizado como cuartel general por el general francés Jean-de-Dieu Soult durante la ocupación francesa de Sevilla.

name: Batalla de Cabezón
description: El 12 de junio de 1808 se produjo el primer intento del general García de la Cuesta de cortar las comunicaciones entre Madrid e Irún, cuando se produjeron sublevaciones en España tras el levantamiento del 2 de mayo.

name: Sitio de Cádiz
description: El asedio de Cádiz es como se le conoce al sitio de Cádiz y San Fernando que tuvo lugar el 5 de febrero de 1810, estableciendo un cerco a la ciudad de Cádiz y San Fernando hasta el 24 de agosto de 1812.

name: Monument to the 100th anniversary of the liberation war against Napoleon
description: Built in 1908, it stands in the places where a parade of troops was held on August 20, 1813, watched by the Austrian Emperor Francis I, the Russian Tsar Alexander I and the Prussian King Frederick William III.

name: Pindulka inn – Battle of Austerlitz
description: Originally established as a coaching inn on the imperial road from  Brno to Olomouc served as a place for changing horses, refreshments and resting passengers. It is said that Napoleon, on the eve of the Battle of  Austerlitz, have dined here.

name: Crossing of the Limmat river memorial (Second battle of Zurich)
description: Place where the french troops under Massena crossed the river during the second battle of Zurich on September 25th 1799.

name: Angriff der Russen bei Poplawi
description: Angriff der Russen bei Poplawi

name: Joseph Bonaparte, roi de Naples
description: Château de Versailles. Artist: Jean-Baptiste Wicar

name: Battle of Schleiz
description: French victory.The Battle of Schleiz took place on 9 October 1806 in Schleiz, Germany between a Prussian-Saxon division under Bogislav Friedrich Emanuel von Tauentzien and a part of Jean-Baptiste Bernadotte's I Corps under the command of Jean-Baptiste Drouet, Comte d'Erlon

name: Battle of Saalfeld
description: French victory. The Battle of Saalfeld took place on 10 October 1806, at which a French force of 12,800 men commanded by Marshal Jean Lannes defeated a Prussian-Saxon force of 8,300 men under Prince Louis Ferdinand.

name: Capitulation of Erfurt
description: At the Capitulation of Erfurt on 16 October 1806, a large body of troops from the Kingdom of Prussia under Lieutenant General the Prince of Orange surrendered to Marshal Joachim Murat of France, at the city of Erfurt (now in Germany).

name: Batalla de Arroyomolinos
description: On 28 October 1811 an allied force under General Rowland Hill trapped and defeated a French force under General Jean-Baptiste Girard.

name: Centro de inerpretaçao Batallo de Vimeiro
San Francisco El Real
description: Las ruinas escondidas que dejó la explosión de un polvorín provisional en 1812 en Salamanca.

name: Aula de Interpretación Batalla de Arapiles
Batalla de Berezina
description: took place from 26 to 29 November 1812, between Napoleon's Grande Armée and the Imperial Russian Army under Field Marshal Wittgenstein and Admiral Chichagov. Napoleon was retreating toward Poland in chaos after the aborted occupation of Moscow and trying to cross the Berezina River at Borisov.

name: Estampe Bataille d’Espinosa
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme

name: Estampe Prise de Madrid
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme

name: Estampe Bataille de Somosierra
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme

name: Estampe Siege et prise de Saragosse
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme

name: Bataille et prise de Burgos
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme

name: Combat et prise de Ratisbonne
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme

name: Siège et prise de Valence
description: Musée Carnavalet, Paris. Gravure anonyme

name: Sitio de Tarragona
description: Toma francesa tras duro asedio con graves pérdidas.

name: Batalla de La Albuera
description: Combate extremadamente sangriento entre aliados y franceses.

name: Batalla de Chiclana (La Barrosa)
description: Enfrentamiento aliado-francés cerca de Cádiz.

name: Asedio de Ciudad Rodrigo
description: Captura aliada clave para penetrar en el interior.

name: Asedio de Badajoz
description: Asalto aliado extremadamente sangriento.

name: Batalla de Vitoria
description: Derrota decisiva del ejército de José Bonaparte.

name: Batalla de San Marcial
description: Victoria española que aseguró la frontera pirenaica.

name: Batalla de Toulouse
description: Última gran batalla peninsular antes de la caída de Napoleón.

name: Acción de El Bruc
description: Milicias catalanas derrotaron a una columna francesa en acción guerrillera.

name: Emboscada de Valdepeñas
description: Población local y guerrilleros frenaron el avance francés hacia Andalucía.

name: Casa Natal de Juan Martín Díez, el “Empecinado”
description: Nacido el 2 de septiembre de 1775, en la localidad vitícola de Castrillo de Duero, e hijo de labrador.

name: Casa Natal de Francisco Espoz y Mina
description: Nació el 17 de junio de 1781  con el nombre de Francisco Espoz Illundáin, pero fue conocido comoFrancisco Espoz y Mina

name: Casa Natal de Francisco Espoz y Mina
description: Nació el 17 de junio de 1781  con el nombre de Francisco Espoz Illundáin, pero fue conocido comoFrancisco Espoz y Mina

name: Sepulcro de don Julián Sánchez, “El Charro”
description: Encontrándose confinado en Etreros (Segovia) fallece en1832.

name: Levantamiento del 2 de mayo
description: Sublevación popular en Madrid contra la ocupación francesa que desencadenó la guerra de independenca española.

name: Batalla de Bailén
description: Primera gran derrota napoleónica en campo abierto.

name: Batalla de Espinosa de los Monteros
description: Derrota española frente a tropas imperiales en el norte.

name: Batalla de Tudela
description: Derrota española frente a tropas imperiales en el norte.

name: Batalla de Somosierra
description: Derrota española frente a tropas imperiales en el norte.

name: Primer Sitio de Zaragoza
description: Asedio francés rechazado por la resistencia local.

name: Segundo Sitio de Zaragoza
description: Segundo asedio francés a Zaragoza, que cayó tras una heroica resistencia.

name: Batalla de Medellín
description: Victoria francesa en Extremadura.

name: Batalla de Talavera
description: Victoria táctica aliada bajo el mando de Wellington.

name: Batalla de Almonacid
description: Derrota española ante fuerzas francesas.

name: Batalla de Ocaña
description: Gran derrota española que permitió ocupar Andalucía.

name: Batalla de Medina de Rioseco
description: El 14 de julio de 1808 se enfrentaron el Ejército español de los generales García de la Cuesta y Joaquín Blake frente al mariscal Jean-Baptiste Bessières

name: Toma del Fuerte del Rastrillar de Laredo
description: Fue una rápida operación, lograda en apenas dos días debido a la baja moral de los defensores italianos, aliados de Francia.

name: Combate de Turienzo de los Caballeros
description: El 2 de enero de 1809, la retaguardia de las tropas españolas fue alcanzada por la poderosa caballería francesa del general Franceschi.

name: Capitan General García de la Cuesta - Pueblo Natal
description: Capitán General de Castilla la Vieja, Extremadura y Mallorca.Algunos biógrafos asignan su nacimiento en Tudanca y otros en La Lastra, poblado a escasos 1.000 metros de distancia.

name: General Castaños - Lugar de nacimiento
description: El vencedor de Bailén nació en Madrid y murió en la pobreza y no existe un museo 'casa natal' como tal en la capital.

name: Castillo de Trevejo
description: Data del siglo XII y fue destruido en 1811 por las tropas francesas para evitar fuera refugio de guerrilleros.

name: Bratislava Cannonball
description: A cannonball fired in 1809 by Napoleonic troops remains embedded in the Old Town Hall tower.

name: St John's Church
description: The graves of five Napoleonic prisoners of war can be found in St John's Churchyard.

name: Batalla de Fuentes de Oñoro
description: Entre el 3 y el 5 de mayo de 1811 se enfrentó el ejército Británico-Portugués con el ejército francés para disminuir la presión del sitio de la ciudad de Almeida.

name: Batalla de Roliça
description: 17 August 1808, was the first battle fought by the British army during the Peninsular War.

name: Batalla de Vimieiro
description: On 21 August 1808, the British under General Arthur Wellesley (who later became the Duke of Wellington) defeated the French under Major-General Jean-Andoche Junot near the village of Vimeiro.

name: Centro de Interpretação da Batalha do Vimeiro
description: O  é um equipamento do Município da Lourinhã que tem como missão o estudo, a investigação, a interpretação, a preservação, a divulgação e a ativação turística do património histórico-cultural e militar.

name: Segona Batalla de Porto
description: El 12 de maig de 1809 va ser una contesa que va acabar amb la victòria per a l'exèrcit anglo-portuguès comandat per Sir Arthur Wellesley sobre l'exèrcit francès del mariscal Nicolas Jean de Dieu Soult.

name: Batalla de García Hernández
description: Fue una acción militar librada el 23 de julio de 1812 entre la caballería ligera del ejército británico y la caballería francesa.

name: Marshal Murat birthplace and museum
description: Rue Joachim Murat, 46240 Labastide-Murat. His birthplace is now a museum dedicated to his life and career.

name: Marshal Lannes birthplace
description: 104 Av. Jean Lannes, 32700 Lectoure. A commemorative plaque can be seen on the facade of the house where 'Roland of the Grande Armée' was born.

name: Marshal Michel Ney birthplace
description: Bierstraße 13 66740 Saarlouis. The house, known as Maison Ney, houses a restaurant and a small memorial space.

name: Les Défenseurs de Saragosse
description: Thomas Henry Museum. This work was painted in 1893 by the French artist Maurice Orange and depicts the moment when the defenders of the city surrendered their weapons to the French troops on February 20th 1809.

name: José de Palafox
description: Museo del Prado. Obra de Francisco de Goya. Posiblemente este lienzo fue un encargo del general Palafox a Goya. En una carta del pintor al general escrita el 4 de enero de 1815, Goya dice haber terminado satisfactoriamente el retrato.

name: Agustina de Aragón
description: Museo Lázaro Galdiano.  La obra representa a la heroína española Agustina de Aragón (1786-1857).

name: Battlefield of Großbeeren
description: Occurred on August 23rd 1813 in neighboring Blankenfelde and Sputendorf [de] between the Prussian III Corps under Friedrich von Bülow and the Franco-Saxon VII Corps under Jean Reynier.

name: El Pilar no se rinde
description: Museo de Zaragoza. El lienzo muestra un Episodio de la defensa de Zaragoza frente a los franceses. Obra de Episodio de la defensa de Zaragoza frente a los franceses. Federico Jiménez Nicanor.

name: El Dos de Mayo
description: Museo del Prado. Obra de Francisco de Goya. Representa la revuelta popular del 2 de mayo de 1808 en Madrid contra la ocupación francesa.

name: El Tres de Mayo
description: Museo del Prado. Obra de Francisco de Goya. Representa la ejecución de los madrileños que se habían sublevado contra los franceses el día anterior.

name: Batalla de Majadahonda
description: The confrontation took place on August 11, 1812, where Napoleonic troops led by General Anne-François-Charles Trelliard defeated Anglo-Portuguese forces led by George Bock and Benjamin d’Urban.

name: La muerte de Daoíz en el Parque de Artillería de Monteleón
description: Museo Nacional del Romanticismo.  Obra de  Alenza y Nieto, Leonardo. Escena de batalla, desarrollada durante la Guerra de la Independencia. Representa los hechos acaecidos durante el 2 de Mayo de 1808, en el Parque de Artillería de Monteleón.

name: Iglesia del Buen Suceso
description: Durante el Levantamiento del 2 de mayo de 1808 la iglesia sufre graves daños en su fachada e interior. Las luchas que se producen contra las tropas francesas en la Puerta del Sol afectan seriamente a su estructura, a la vez que convierten a la iglesia del Buen Suceso en un símbolo de la resistencia madrileña.

name: Monumento a Daoíz y Velarde
description: Monumento a los héroes del levantamiento del 2 de mayo de 1808 en Madrid.

name: Monumento a Daoíz y Velarde
description: Obra del artista Aniceto Marinas en la ciudad de Segovia y una de las más notables que produjo el arte español de su época.

name: Muerte de Velarde el Dos de Mayo de 1808
description: Museo de Historia de Madrid. Obra de Manuel Castellano.

name: Lugar de la escena 'Malasaña' del pintor Eugenio Álvarez Dumont
description: En esta esquina tiene escena el cuadro del pintor Eugenio Álvarez Dumont, en la cual represnta a Juan Manuel Malasaña  asestando un fatídico golpe mortal al dragón francés que acababa de asesinar a su hija, la bordadora Manuela Malasaña.

name: El juramento de las tropas del Marqués de la Romana
description: Museo del Ejército. Obra de Manuel Castellano. El lienzo representa el momento en que las tropas españolas a las órdenes del marqués de la Romana, Pedro Caro y Sureda, prestaron juramento antes de embarcar hacia Dinamarca para proteger a Dinamarca, que era aliada de Napoleón.

name: Museo del Ejército
description: Exhibe una interesante colección de objetos de la guerra de la independencia española.

name: Malasaña y su hija se baten contra los franceses
description: Museo de Zaragoza. Obra de Eugenio Álvarez Dumont. El lienzo forma parte de la colección de pintura del siglo XIX del museo. Esta obra narra la lucha y muerte de Juan Malasaña y su hija Manuela durante los levantamientos del 2 de mayo de 1808 en Madrid.

name: Museo de Historia de Madrid
description: En la planta primera, se ubica la exposición de la sala Madrid 1700-1814, Centro Ilustrado de Poder, cuyo espacio final es ocupado por los hechos de la Guerra de la Independencia y los acontecimientos del Dos de Mayo en Madrid, con el cuadro de Francisco de Goya, «Alegoria de la villa de Madrid» y las estampas satíricas de Pepe Botella, entre otras.

name: Battle of Gospic
description: On May 21st 1809, Marmont located Rebrovich's forces deployed behind the Lika River near Gospić.

name: Baionde position (Battle of Arcole)
description: Massena pushes back the Austrians commanded by Briabeck from Bionde on November 15th and 16th 1796.

name: The Coronation of Emperor Alexander I
description: The coronation of Emperor Alexander I and Elizabeth Alexeyevna took place on 15 September 1801 in the Dormition Cathedral of the Moscow Kremlin.

name: The St. Martin Church in Aspern
description: The St. Martin Church in Aspern served as a critical, fiercely contested strongpoint during the Battle of Aspern-Essling (May 21–22, 1809), where Napoleon suffered his first major personal defeat against Austrian forces led by Archduke Charles. The church, surrounded by a graveyard, was destroyed during the intense fighting and later rebuilt.

name: Adige river crossing at Albaredo (Battle of Arcole)
description: Bonaparte ordered General Jean Joseph Guieu to cross the adiage river at Albaredo to march towards the Austrian left wing at Arcole.

name: Battlefield of Aspern-Essling
description: It was the first time Napoleon had been personally defeated in a major battle, as well as his first battle defeat in 10 years since the siege of Acre, and his first battle defeat as head of state, although he did suffer a tactical defeat in the Battle of Caldiero and in the Second Battle of Bassano. Archduke Charles drove out the French but fell short of destroying their army.

name: Battle of Wagram
description: The Battle of Wagram was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on July 5–6, 1809, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

name: Burial site of Alvinczi József
description: Field Marshal of the Austrian Empire. He defeated Bonaparte at Bassano on 6 November and Caldiero on 12 November 1796 and was defeated at Arcole ont November 17th.

name: Vendôme Column
description: Erected by Napoleon in 1810 to celebrate the victory at Austerlitz, this 44-meter bronze column in Place Vendôme was modeled after Trajan's Column in Rome. It was cast from 1,200 cannons captured from the combined armies of Europe.

name: Real Fábrica de Porcelana de Madrid
description: In 1808, Napoleon's armies converted this Royal Porcelain Factory into a barracks. In August 1812, it was captured by Wellington's troops, who ordered that, once the troops left the fort, all the fortifications of El Retiro Park be destroyed, including this Royal Porcelain Factory, which had made Madrid porcelain a favorite among the nobility and royal houses of much of Europe.

name: Segundo Sitio de Astorga
description: El segundo sitio se produjo entre el 15 de junio y el 19 de agosto de 1812 y tuvo como resultado la recuperación de la ciudad por parte española.

name: Battlefield of Leipzig
description: Known as the Battle of the Nations, was the largest battle in the Napoleonic Wars. Napoleon's defeat by a coalition of Austrian, Prussian, Russian, and Swedish forces led to his first exile.

name: Château de Malmaison
description: The private residence of Napoleon and Joséphine de Beauharnais. Purchased in 1799, it served as the seat of the French government alongside the Tuileries Palace. The château now houses a museum dedicated to the couple.

name: Battle of Mehrnbach
description: Took place on October 30th 1805, shortly after the surrender of the Austrian troops in Ulm, from where the road to Vienna lay open to the Grande Armée.

name: Battle of Wischau
description: Occurred on November 28th 1805 between the Russian and French armies. The conflict resulted in a minor Russian success.

name: Painting - Bailen's Surrender
description: The Surrender of Bailén (Spanish: La Rendición de Bailén) is an 1864 history painting by the Spanish artist José Casado del Alisal. It commemorates a scene from the Battle of Bailén during the early stages of the Peninsular War, the surrender of a French Army under Pierre Dupont to Spanish forces on 19 July 1808.

name: Elba - Villa dei Mulini
description: Napoleon's primary residence during his first exile on the island of Elba (1814-1815). From here, he governed the island and planned his dramatic return to France. The villa overlooks the harbor of Portoferraio.

name: Battlefield of Quatre Bras
description: Fought on 16 June 1815, as a preliminary engagement to the decisive Battle of Waterloo that occurred two days later.

name: Tomb of Marie-Louise, Empress of the French
description: Marie Louise fell ill on 9 December 1847. Her condition worsened for the next few days. On 17 December, she passed out. The cause of death was determined to be pleurisy.

name: Parador de Lerma - Ducal Palace
description: The Parador de Lerma (Burgos), a former 17th-century Ducal Palace, housed Napoleon Bonaparte in 1809 during the Peninsular War, using it as his base and lodgings. It is said that he set up his offices in what is now room 313.

name: Bonaparte's HQ batlle of Arcole
description: Napoléon Bonaparte's headquarters during the battle of Arcole and the following day.

name: Bonaparte's HQ before batlle of Arcole
description: On November 14, 1796, during the Italian campaign, Napoleon moved his headquarters to Villafranca and the night before the Battle of Arcole he stayed at the Comini Palace, now called Caffé Fantoni.

name: Battlefield of Corunna / Elviña
description: The Battle of Corunna (or A Coruña, La Corunna, La Coruña or La Corogne), in Spain known as Battle of Elviña, took place on 16 January 1809, when a French corps under Marshal of the Empire Jean de Dieu Soult attacked a British army under Lieutenant-General Sir John Moore. The battle took place during the Peninsular War, which was part of the wider Napoleonic Wars.

name: Austrian barracks at Arcole
description: During the battle of Arcole, this building was used as barracks by Austrian troops and subsequently targeted by the French army. The holes caused by French soldiers' bullets are still visible on the south façade.

name: Citadel of Madrid
description: The citadel of Madrid was a star fort with three fortified enceintes[1] constructed by the French Army during the Peninsular War, in the grounds of the Palacio del Buen Retiro, on the Retiro heights,[note 1] outside the eastern gate of the city of Madrid.

name: Battle of Arcole Museum
description: The 'G.A. Antonelli' Napoleonic Museum in Arcole recounts, through artifacts and period prints, the Napoleonic epic and key moments in Napoleon Bonaparte's life. It contains a rich collection of paintings, prints, copper and steel engravings, lithographs and aquatints, manifestos and proclamations, bulletins of the Grande Armée, and period documents.

name: Battle of Pyramids of Giza
description: The Battle of the Pyramids on July 21, 1798 was a decisive French victory during Napoleon's Egyptian campaign. Napoleon famously addressed his troops: 'Soldiers, from the height of these pyramids, forty centuries look down upon you.'

name: Longwood House, Saint Helena
description: Napoleon's final residence during his exile on the remote island of Saint Helena. He lived here from 1815 until his death on May 5, 1821. The house has been preserved as a museum by the French government.

name: Musée de l'Armée
description: Located within Les Invalides in Paris, this military museum houses one of the world's largest collections of military artifacts, with extensive Napoleonic galleries featuring weapons, uniforms, personal items, and the Emperor's iconic bicorne hat.

name: Musée Wellington Waterloo
description: The Wellington Museum in Waterloo, Belgium, is located in the house where the Duke of Wellington, spent the night before and after the Battle of Waterloo (18 June 1815).[1]  The museum contains information about the Duke of Wellington, the Waterloo Campaign, the main phases of the Battle of Waterloo a Gallery and contemporary military artifacts from the armies that fought in the battle.

name: Santa Cruz Palace
description: During the Peninsular War it was closed to students and none other than the Commander-in-Chief of the British Army, Lord Wellington, stayed there after expelling the French troops from the city.

name: Kagran inn
description: Napoleon was hosted a this site during the battle of ASPERN-ESSLING (21 and 22 May 1809)

name: San Carlos Garden- Gen. Moore's Tomb
description: It is a 'hortus conclusus' created around the tomb of the British general Sir John Moore, who died in the battle of Elviña against the French in 1809.

name: Quinta de los Duques de Pastrana
description: In December 1808, Napoleon stayed at the Quinta de los Duques de Pastrana. At that time,  in this area was located a small town outside Madrid called Chamartín de la Rosa, frequented by many French people. Its very name has Gallic origins, as there was a tavern in the village called ‘Chez Martin’ whose name evolved into Chamartín, giving the district its name

name: Battlefield of Jena
description: On October 14, 1806, Napoleon crushed the Prussian army at the Battle of Jena-Auerstedt. This decisive victory led to the French occupation of most of Prussia and cemented Napoleon's control over Central Europe.

name: Painting - Scotland forever
description: Lady Butler's depiction of the Scots Greys famous charge at the Battle of Waterloo. In actuality, it appears that they were unable to build up the momentum of a gallop, because the broken ground obliged them to advance at a canter.

name: Painting - The year of the famine in Madrid
location: Museo de Historia de Madrid, Madrid
description: Exhibited at the 'Museo de Historia de Madrid'. Painted by José Aparicio, often associated with the 1811–1812 famine period, with some sources mentioning it was completed around 1818.'The Year of the Famine in Madrid' (Spanish: El año del hambre en Madrid) depicts the dire food shortages in Madrid during the French occupation from 1811–1812. The oil on canvas portrays the misery of the population, with hungry, ragged individuals receiving food from French soldiers.

name: Ronco Bridge over the Adige river (Battle of Arcole)
description: During the three-day battle, the town of Ronco all'Adige received the largest number of French troops and Austrian prisoners. Napoleon had built a pontoon bridge across the Adige. Ronco's position allowed him to retreat quickly should bad news arrive from the Adige Valley.

name: Battle of Arcis-sur-Aube
description: The Battle of Arcis-sur-Aube (20-21 March 1814) saw an Imperial French army under Napoleon face a much larger Allied army led by Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg during the War of the Sixth Coalition.

name: École Militaire
description: The prestigious military academy in Paris where the young Napoleon studied from 1784 to 1785. He graduated in just one year rather than the usual two or three, becoming a second lieutenant of artillery at age 16.

name: Battlefield of Bautzen
description: (20–21 May 1813), a combined Prusso-Russian army, retreating after their defeat at Lützen and massively outnumbered, was pushed back by Napoleon but escaped destruction.

name: Battle of Auerstaedt
description: This battle pitted the first Prussian army under King Frederick William III of Prussia against part of the French army led by Louis Nicolas Davout on October 14, 1806, parallel to the Battle of Jena, which was fought on the same date.

name: Battle of Friedland
description: Fought on June 14, 1807, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Russian army under General Levin August von Bennigsen.

name: Battle of Ulm
description: The Battle of Ulm was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on October 16–19, 1805, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

name: Battle of Trafalgar
description: The Battle of Trafalgar was fought on October 21, 1805, between the British Royal Navy and the combined fleets of the French and Spanish Navies.

name: Battlefield of Eylau
description: The Battle of Eylau (also known as the Battle of Preussisch-Eylau) was a bloody and strategically inconclusive battle on 7 and 8 February [O.S. 26 and 27 Jan.] 1807 between Napoleon's Grande Armée and the Imperial Russian Army under the command of General Levin August von Bennigsen

name: Battle of Ligny
description: Fought on 16 June 1815French troops of the Armée du Nord under the command of Napoleon I defeated part of a Prussian army under Field Marshal Blücher.

name: Battle of Pultusk
description: Took place on December 26th 1806 Despite their strong numerical superiority and artillery, the Russians under the lead of General of Cavalry Levin von Benningsen underwent heavy French attacks by Marshal Jean Lannes.

name: Battle of Golymin
description: Fought on the same day as Pultusk, December 26th, this was another intense winter engagement between French and Russian forces in Poland.

name: Battle of Heilsberg
description: Took place on 10 June 1807. Napoleon confronted Russian general Bennigsen and his army. The battle was a bloody engagement that lasted for several hours, with heavy casualties on both sides.

name: Siege of Danzig
description: The Siege of Danzig (now Gdańsk, Poland)by French forces under Marshal Lefebvre, acting as the corps commander of the Grande Armée, during the War of the Fourth Coalition.

name: Battle of Ostrolenka
description: Fought on 16 February 1807 between a French force under General of Division Anne Jean Marie René Savary and a Russian force under Lieutenant General Ivan Essen.

name: Battle of Znaim
description: Following defeat at the Battle of Wagram, Archduke Charles retreated north into Bohemia hoping to regroup his battered forces. The French army had also suffered in the battle and did not give immediate pursuit. But two days after the battle, Napoleon ordered his troops north intending to defeat the Austrians once and for all.

name: Walcheren Campaign
description: It was an unsuccessful British expedition to the Kingdom of Holland in 1809 intended to open another front in the Austrian Empire's struggle with France during the War of the Fifth Coalition.

name: Attack of Flushing
description: The British had captured Flushing on the day of his arrival to the war zone after a ferocious bombardment, and the surrounding towns on August 15th.

name: Battlefield of Arcole
description: The Battle of Arcole or Battle of Arcola (15–17 November 1796) was fought between French and Austrian forces 25 kilometres (16 mi) southeast of Verona during the War of the First Coalition, a part of the French Revolutionary Wars.

name: Battle of Ebelsberg
description: A bloody French assault to take a key bridge over the Traun River, clearing the way to Vienna.

name: Captain Antonio Costa grave
description: Grave of Captain Antonio Costa, member of Spaniard expedition to Denmark in 1807.

name: Battlefield Combat of the Côa
description: Took place on 24 July 1810, on the banks of the Côa River, in the borderlands between Portugal and Spain. It was fought between a French division under Marshal Auguste de Marmont and a British division under General Robert Craufurd.

name: Bridge over the Côa River
description: Rather than retreat and cross the river as ordered by Wellington, Craufurd chose to engage but was forced to continue its withdrawal further that night.

name: Church of Our Lady
description: In September 1807, the cathedral was destroyed during the bombardment of Copenhagen by a British Royal Navy fleet under Admiral James Gambier

name: Landing at Vedbæk
description: British troops landed at Vedbæk on August 16th 1807 and began besieging Copenhage.

name: Battlefield of Køge
description: Fought on 29 August 1807 between British troops besieging Copenhagen and Danish militia raised on Sjælland

name: Battlefield of Classens Have
description: Fought on August 31st 1807 between British troops and Danish volunteers

name: Battle of Guttstadt-Deppen
description: A violent engagement in the Polish/Prussian region during the prelude to the final campaign of the Fourth Coalition.

name: Musée de l'Empéri
description: It is a historic castle contains faithful representations of the various army corps from the Napoleonic Wars

name: Museum of the Patriotic War of 1812
description: Opened in honor of the 200 Anniversary of the war. The exhibition consists of several thematic sections and is arranged in chronological order: from the events before the War of 1812 to foreign campaigns of Russian Army in 1813–1814.

name: Marshall Davout's Birthplace
description: The birthplace of Marshal Davout, one of Napoleon's most trusted commanders.

name: Second Battle of Polotsk
description: It tool place from October 18th to the 20th during Napoleon's invasion of Russia.In this encounter the Russians under General Peter Wittgenstein attacked and defeated a Franco-Bavarian force under Laurent Gouvion Saint-Cyr.

name: Battle of Blanc-Nez and Gris-Nez
description: Fought on 18 July 1805 between a combined flotilla of the Batavian Navy and French Imperial Navy under Carel Hendrik Ver Huell and a Royal Navy fleet under Lord Keith during the War of the Third Coalition.

name: Battle of Diamond Rock
description: Took place between 31 May and 2 June 1805 during the Napoleonic Wars, when a Franco-Spanish force dispatched under Captain Julien Cosmao was able to retake Diamond Rock, on the approach to Fort-de-France, from the British forces that had occupied it over a year before.

name: Battle of Cape Finisterre
description: On July 22nd 1805, the British fleet under Admiral Robert Calder fought an indecisive Naval battle against the combined Franco-Spanish fleet which was returning from the West Indies.

name: Battle of Donauwörth
description: This was the first engagement of Napoleon's 1805 Austrian campaign. French forces under marshals Joachim Murat and Nicolas Soult beat an Austrian army corps under Kienmayer on October 7th 1805 at Donauwörth and crossed the Danube.

name: Battle of Wertingen
description: On October 8th 1805, French forces led by Marshals Murat and Jean Lannes attacked a small Austrian corps commanded by Feldmarschall-Leutnant Franz Xaver von Auffenberg.

name: Battle of Gunzburg
description: On October 9th 1805,Marshall Ney ordered a French division to seize a crossing over the Danube River at Günzburg in the face of a Ha  bsburg Austrian army led by Feldmarschall-Leutnant Karl Mack von Lieberich.

name: Battle of Haslach-Jungingen (Albeck)
description: Also known as the Battle of Albeck, fought on October 11th 1805 at Ulm-Jungingen north of Ulm at the Danube between French and Austrian forces, was part of the Ulm campaign.

name: Battle of Memmingen
description: It occurred on 14 October that year and culminated in the surrender of Karl Spangen to Jean-de-Dieu Soult's 4th Army Corps.

name: Battle of Elchingen
description: Fought on 14th October 1805, French forces under Michel Ney routed an Austrian corps led by Johann Sigismund Riesch.

name: Battle of Verona
description: Fought on October 18th 1805 between the French Army of Italy under the command of André Masséna and an Austrian army led by Archduke Charles. (image:Djmaschek)

name: Ponte di Castelvecchio
description: In the early hours of October 18th 1805, Masséna led his storming column silently onto the Castelvecchio Bridge starting the Battle of Verona.

name: Battle of Caldiero
description: Took place on October 30th 1805, pitting the French Armée d'Italie under Marshal André Masséna against an Austrian army under the command of Archduke Charles.

name: Invasion of Surinam
description: The invasion of Surinam, on April 25th 1804, was a British military campaign which resulted in the capture and occupation of the Dutch colony of Surinam in 1804.

name: Battle of Mohilew
description: Also known as the Battle of Saltanovka, took place on 23 July 1812 during the early stages of the 1812 French invasion of Russia.

name: Battle of Klyastitsy
description: Also called the Battle of Yakubovo or the Battle of Oboyarshchina, was a series of military engagements that took place on 30 July [O.S. 18 July] 1812 – 1 August 1812.

name: Battlefield of Aboukir
description: Fought on July 25th 1799 between the French Army of the Orient under Napoleon Bonaparte and an Ottoman army under Mustafa Pasha.

name: Battlefield of Abensberg
description: Took place on April 20th 1809 between a Franco-German force under the command of Emperor Napoleon I of France and a reinforced Austrian corps led by Feldmarschall-Leutnant Archduke Louis of Austria.

name: Battlefield of Echmühl
description: Fought on 21–22 April 1809, was the turning point of the 1809 Campaign.

name: Battlefield of Landshut
description: Took place on April 21st 1809 between the French, Württembergers (VIII Corps) and Bavarians (VII Corps) under Napoleon, and the Austrians under the General Johann von Hiller.

name: Battlefield of Ratisbon
description: Also called the Battle of Regensburg, fought on 23 April 1809, between the army of the French Empire, led by Napoleon I, and that of the Austrian Empire, led by Archduke Charles.

name: Battlefield of Neumarkt-Sankt Veit
description: On April 24th 1809 saw a Franco-Bavarian force led by Marshal Jean-Baptiste Bessières face an Austrian Empire army commanded by Johann von Hiller.

name: Battlefield of Radzymin
description: Took place on 25 April 1809, during the brief Polish–Austrian War. The battle occurred at Radzymin, some 20 kilometres (12 mi) north-east of Warsaw.

name: Battlefield of La Piave
description: Fought on May 8th 1809 between the Franco-Italian army under the command of Eugène de Beauharnais and an Austrian army led by Archduke John of Austria. (Image by Djmaschek)

name: Battlefield of Wörgl
description: Fought on May 13th 1809, when a Bavarian force under French Marshal François Joseph Lefebvre attacked an Austrian Empire detachment commanded by Johann Gabriel Chasteler de Courcelles.

name: Kufstein fortress
description: The 576-man Bavarian garrison surrendered to the Bavarian General Bernhard Deroy on May 11th 1809.

name: Battlefield of Tarvis
description: The two day battle took place from May 16th to May 17th 1809.

name: Forte di Malborghetto
description: The fortress was stormed by French troops on May 15th to May 17th 1809.

name: Monument to Friedrich Hensel
description: The monument was erected in memory of Friedrich Hensel, who was killed in the Battle of Malborghetto.

name: Storming of Forti del Predil
description: From May 15th to the 18th 1809, the Franco-Italian army of Eugène de Beauharnais attacked Austrian Empire forces under Albert Gyulai.

name: Monument to Captain Johann Hermann von Hermannsdorf
description: E rected in memory of Johann Hermann von Hermannsdorf, who was killed in the Battle of Malborghetto.

name: Battlefield of Linz-Urfahr
description: The Battle of Linz-Urfahr was fought on May 17th 1809, where the Austrian Empire Army fought against troops from two of Emperor Napoleon's allies, the Kingdom of Württemberg and the Kingdom of Saxony.

name: Pfennigberg hill
description: The Württemberg Duke Louis Jäger zu Pferde Regiment and the Saxon Hussars overran an Austrian artillery battery at Pfennigberg hill, capturing six guns.

name: Battle of Sankt Michael
description: On May 25th 1809, Paul Grenier's French corps crushed Franz Jellacic's Austrian division at Sankt Michael in Obersteiermark, Austria.

name: Battle of the Basque Roads
description: The battle lasted from April 11th to the 24th 1809 and was a major Naval battle of the Napoleonic Wars, fought in the narrow Basque Roads at the mouth of the Charente River on the Biscay coast of France.(image by BarretBonden)

name: Battlefield of Raab/Győr
description: It was fought on June 14th 1809 between Franco-Italian forces and Habsburg forces. Most of the Habsburg army consisted of poorly trained Landwehr and militia.

name: Battlefield of Graz (Gratz)
description: took place on June 24th to 26th 1809 between an Austrian corps commanded by Ignaz Gyulai and a French division led by Jean-Baptiste Broussier who was later reinforced by a corps under Auguste Marmont.

name: Saint Leonhard Church and cemetery
description: Austrian strongpoint at the battle of Graz attacked by Colonel Jean Hugues Gambin with 1,200 men.

name: Battlefield Combat of Korneuburg
description: It was a relatively minor rearguard action fought by Austrian VI Korps of the Kaiserlich-königliche Hauptarmee under Johann von Klenau against elements of the French IV Corps of the Armée d'Allemagne, under the command of Claude Legrand.

name: Louis Braun
description: The baroque Innsbruck hospital church on Maria-Theresien-Strasse in the oil canvas by Louis Braun still standing today, was at the centre of the fighting.

name: Äussere Taverne
description: Napoleon established his quarters on October 13th, 1805. at this building.

name: Palazzo Salmatoris
description: Palazzo Salmatoris is also known as the 'Peace Palace' because on April 26th 1796 it saw the signing of the armistice of Cherasco between Napoleon I and the Savoy kingdom.

name: St. Peter and Paul Church
description: At Battle of Haslach-Jungingen the church was held by the 9th Light infanty of Dupont's Division.The Austrian assault was slowed at this place.

name: Battlefield of Gefrees
description: Fought on July 8th 1809 during the War of the Fifth Coalition between a joint force of Austrians and Brunswickers under the command of General Kienmayer and a French force under the command of General Junot.

name: Hassenhausen Museum
description: At Battle of Haslach-Jungingen the church was held by the 9th Light infanty of Dupont's Division.The Austrian assault was slowed at this place.

name: Battlefield of Hollabrunn
description: Was a rearguard action fought on July 9th 1809 by Austrian VI Korps of the Kaiserlich-königliche Hauptarmee under Johann von Klenau against elements of the French IV Corps of the Armée d'Allemagne, under the command of André Masséna

name: Battlefield of Mökern (Dannigkow)
description: also known as the Battle of Dannigkow was a series of heavy clashes on April 5th 1813 between allied Prusso-Russian troops under Russian commander Peter Wittgenstein and Napoleonic French forces under Eugène de Beauharnais.

name: Battlefield of Luneburg
description: On April 2nd 1813, Allied Russians and Prussians were victorious over a French and Saxon division.

name: Battlefield of Luckau
description: Prussian and Russian forces under General Friedrich Wilhelm von Bülow defeated part of a French-Allied corps under Marshal Nicolas Oudinot on June 4th 1813.

name: Battlefield of Lutzen
description: Prussian and Russian forces under General Friedrich Wilhelm von Bülow defeated part of a French-Allied corps under Marshal Nicolas Oudinot on May 2nd 1813.

name: Battlefield of Hanau
description: was fought from October 30th to 31st 1813 between Karl Philipp von Wrede's Austro-Bavarian corps and Napoleon's retreating French army.

name: Battlefield of Haynau
description: Fought on May 26th 1813, between Prussian cavalry under the command of General Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher and a French infantry division under the command of General Nicolas Joseph Maison

name: Blucher monument at Chojnów
description: To commemorate Blücher's first victory in Silesia, a monument was erected in the Chojnów market square with the inscription: 'The glorious victory of Blücher's cavalry at Haynau, 26 May 1813'.

name: Grobbeeren Obelisk
description: The monument commemorating the victorious battles against Napoleon was designed in the form of a Gothic tabernacle by the Royal Prussian Iron Foundry  in 1817–18.

name: Grobbeeren Churchyard
description: Churchyard which inspired the scene of the painting by Carl Rochling.

name: Battlefield of Katzbach
description: Fought on August 26th 1813, was a major battle of the Napoleonic Wars between the forces of the First French Empire under Marshal MacDonald and a Russo-Prussian army of the Sixth Coalition under Prussian Marshal Graf von Blücher.

name: Battlefield of Dresden
description: Fought on August 26th - 27th 1813, was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars. The battle took place around the city of Dresden in modern-day Germany, then the capital of the Kingdom of Saxony.

name: Battlefield of Hagelberg (Lübnitz)
description: The Battle of Hagelberg took place on August 27th 1813, following the Battle of Grossbeeren and in the run-up to the Battle of Leipzig.

name: Monument Battle of Hagelberg
description: Monument dating from 1848/49.Battle of Hagelberg was erected in 1817 to commemorate the Battle of Hagelberg, which took place on August 27th 1813, following the Battle of Grossbeeren and in the run-up to the Battle of Leipzig.

name: Battlefield of Kulm
description: The Battle of Kulm took place on August 29th - 30th 1813 when a French corps under General Dominique Vandamme attacked Alexander Osterman-Tolstoy's Russian corps.

name: Russian Memorial of the Battle of 1813
description: built from 1835 to 1837 to commemorate the heroism of the Russian Guards regiments and their commander, General Ostermann-Tolsté, who on the first day of the Battle of Chlumec and Přestanov (August 29th, 1813) stopped the advance of the French corps of General Vandamme at the cost of significant losses.

name: Mass grave of the victims of the battle of Chlumec and Přestanov
description: It is a place of worship and a war grave in the countryside near Přestanov. It contains the remains of approximately 10,000 soldiers who fell in the battle of Chlumec and Přestanov (29–30 August 1813).

name: Battlefield of Dennewitz
description: took place on September 6th 1813 between French forces commanded by Marshal Michel Ney and the Allied Army of the North commanded by Crown Prince Charles John of Sweden, Friedrich Wilhelm von Bülow and Bogislav von Tauentzien.

name: Battlefield of Wartenburg
description: Took place on October 3rd 1813 between the French IV Corps commanded by General Henri Gatien Bertrand and the Allied Army of Silesia, principally the I Corps of General Ludwig von Yorck with the personal participation of the army commander, Gebhard L. von Blücher.

name: Monument commemorating the Battle of Dennewitz
description: Erected in 1913, the 100th anniversary of the Battle of Dennewitz on September 6, 1813, during the Wars of Liberation.

name: Battlefield of Sacile
description: Also known as the Battle of Fontana Fredda, saw an Austrian army commanded by Archduke John of Austria defeat a Franco-Italian army led by Eugène de Beauharnais and force it to retreat on April 16th 1809.

name: Batalla de la Poza de Sta Isabel
description: Also known as the capture of the Rosily squadron, took place on June 14th 1808 in Cádiz following the Dos de Mayo Uprising against French troops in Madrid.

name: Action of 4 April 1808
description: The Royal Navy warships HMS Alceste, HMS Mercury and HMS Grasshopper intercepted and defeated a large Spanish convoy protected by twenty gunboats and a train of batteries close to shore.

name: Action of 2 June 1807
description: During the British invasions of the River Plate between a Royal Navy schooner and two Spanish small vessels, a privateer sloop and naval felucca, at the mouth of the Paraná Guazú.

name: La Romana's division evacuation
description: On August 10th 1808 the Spaniard troops commanded by the Marquis of La Romana defected from the armies of the First French Empire.

name: Embarque del Marqués de La Romana y sus tropas
description: Museo Nacional del Romanticismo. Obra de Juan Rodríguez Jiménez que escenifica el momento en el que el marqués de la Romana, en el centro de la escena, arenga a las tropas, preparadas para embarcar en los navíos ingleses.

name: Battle of Nazareth
description: Musée d'Arts de Nantes. Canvas by Antoine-Jean Gros depicting the Battle of Nazareth.

name: Battle of Cape Santa Maria
description: Took place on October 5th 1804. In this confrontation, a British squadron under the command of Commodore Graham Moore attacked and defeated a Spanish squadron led by Brigadier Don José de Bustamante.

name: Action of 23 August 1806
description: In the engagement on August 23 1806, the Spanish frigate Pomona was captured by the British frigates HMS Anson and HMS Arethusa under the commands of Captain Charles Lydiard and Charles Brisbane.
commanders: Pedro Pablo de Sanguineto y Basso

name: Battle of Cape Ortegal
description: On November 4th 1805 took place the final action of the Trafalgar campaign, and was fought between a squadron of the Royal Navy and a remnant of the fleet that had been defeated earlier at the Battle of Trafalgar.

name: Battle of Alvøen
description: On May 16th 1808 the British frigate HMS Tartar and five Norwegian gunboats off Bergen clashed as part of the Gunboat War.

name: The Naval Battle of Alvøen
description: The Kode Bergen Art Museum houeses this canvas by Johan Christian Claussen Dahl painted in 1808

name: Battle of Anholt
description: Fought on March 27th 1811, during the Gunboat War, it was an attempt by the Dano-Norwegians to recapture Anholt, a small Danish island off the coast of Jutland which the British had captured in 1809.

name: Battle of Copenhagen (1807)
description: Also known as the Bombardment of Copenhagen, it occurred between August 16th and September 7th 1807. British forces bombarded the Danish capital in order to capture or destroy the Royal Dano-Norwegian Navy.

name: Battle of Copenhagen (1801)
description: Also known as the First Battle of Copenhagen, was a naval battle in which a British fleet fought and defeated a smaller force of the Dano-Norwegian Navy anchored near Copenhagen on April 2nd 1801.

name: Battle of Grand Port
description: Was fought from August 20th to the 27th 1810 between the British and French navies over the harbour of Grand Port, Isle de France, as part of the Mauritius campaign.

name: Combat du Grand Port, 23 août 1810
description: The Musée national de la Marine de Paris houses this canvas by Pierre-Julien Gilbert, where the HMS Magicienne and the HMS Sirius being scuttled by fire

name: Invasion of Isle Bonaparte
description: The British invasion of the French island of Réunion, then known as Isle Bonaparte, was a successful operation by the British Royal Navy and Army against the French garrison. The island was captured on July 8th 1810.

name: Raid on Griessie
description: The British invasion of the French island of Réunion, then known as Isle Bonaparte, was a successful operation by the British Royal Navy and Army against the French garrison. The island was captured on July 8th 1810.

name: Battle of Jobourg
description: It was a minor naval engagement on March 26th to 27th 1814 between the British and French navies during the last weeks of the War of the Sixth Coalition.

name: Battle of Lissa
description: Also known as the Battle of Vis, was fought between a British frigate squadron and a much larger squadron of French and Italian frigates and smaller vessels on Wednesday, March 13th 1811 during the Adriatic campaign.

name: Battle of Zealand Point
description: It was fought off Zealand on March 22nd 1808 during the Gunboat War. Three ships of the line and one frigate of the Royal Navy intercepted and destroyed a Danish ship of the line.

name: Battle of Tamatave
description: It was fought off Tamatave in the Indian Ocean between British and French frigate squadrons during the Napoleonic Wars. The action was the final engagement of the Mauritius campaign of 1809/1811.

name: Memorial Zealand Point Battle
description: At the Odden churchyard was erected a monument commemorating the battle fought on March 22nd 1808.

name: Battle of Les Sables-d'Olonne
description: It was a minor naval battle fought on February 23rd 1809 off the town of Les Sables-d'Olonne on the Biscay Coast of France between a French Navy squadron of three frigates and a larger British squadron of ships of the line.

name: Battle of the Nile (Aboukir Bay)
description: Also known as the Battle of Aboukir Bay, was fought between the Royal Navy and the French Navy at Aboukir Bay in Egypt between August 1st and 3rd 1798. It was the climax of the Mediterranean campaign of 1798.

name: Battle of the Nile (Aboukir Bay)
description: Also known as the Battle of Aboukir Bay, was fought between the Royal Navy and the French Navy at Aboukir Bay in Egypt between August 1st and 3rd 1798. It was the climax of the Mediterranean campaign of 1798.

name: Battle of Brienne
description: On January 29th 1814,an Imperial French army led by Emperor Napoleon attack Prussian and Russian forces commanded by Prussian Field Marshal Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher.

name: Musée Napoléon de Brienne
description: Arms, ammunition, uniforms and complete kit from these battle sites complete the exhibition. Of particular interest is the statue of Napoleon above the entrance to the school and the plaque listing the names of the pupils who graduated from the school between 1776 and 1790.

name: Military School in Brienne
description: Venue of the Military School in Brienne, where Napoleon Bonaparte spent part of his youth, from May 1779 to October 1784.

name: Battlefield of Castiglione
description: A battle fought between French forces under the First Consul Napoleon Bonaparte and Austrian forces near the city of Alessandria, in Piedmont, Italy.

name: Monte Medolano
description: Locally called 'Monte della Barcaccia'. Around al 10am Marmont moved his artillery onto Mount Medolano while Augereau's division begins the attack in the central plain.

name: Siege of Wittenberg
description: By 1813, the fortress had become an important assembly point and supply depot for the French fighting in eastern Germany. On January 15th 1814, the fortress was surrendered to the allied forces.

name: Battlefield of Hoyerswerda
description: It was a French victory on May 23rd 1813, which encouraged Marshal Oudinot to advance towards Berlin, after a Prussian attack on his positions was repulsed.

name: Battlefield of Mondovi
description: It was fought on April 21st 1796 between the French army of Napoleon Bonaparte and the army of the Kingdom of Sardinia-Piedmont led by Michelangelo Alessandro Colli-Marchi.

name: Battlefield of Dego
description: The Second Battle of Dego was fought on April 14th and 15th 1796 during the French Revolutionary Wars between French forces and Austro-Sardinian forces.

name: Battlefield of Halle (1806)
description: On October 17th 1806, a French corps led by Jean-Baptiste Bernadotte fought the Prussian Reserve under Eugene Frederick Henry, Duke of Württemberg.
commanders: Eugene Frederick Henry, Duke of Württemberg

name: Battlefield of Halle (1813)
description: The Battle of Halle was a spring campaign battle of the German War of Liberation, which took place on May 2nd 1813, at Halle on the Saale River in Germany.

name: Alcazar de Segovia
description: Fue ocupado por las tropas francesas, quienes utilizaron el edificio como fortaleza y prisión, sufriendo daños considerables en su estructura y tesoros artísticos

name: Battlefield of Wavre
description: It was fought on June 18th and 19th 1815 and was the final major military action of the Hundred Days campaign and the Napoleonic Wars.

name: Monument to General Gérard
description: It was fought on June 18th and 19th 1815 and was the final major military action of the Hundred Days campaign and the Napoleonic Wars.

name: La Bawette
description: Position occupied by the Prussian reserve cavalry formed by Lottum's units and Broke's division.

name: Église Saint-Jean-Baptiste
description: The Saint-Jean-Baptiste church in Wavre still bears the marks of the battle of June 18, 1815. A French cannonball is embedded in one of the pillars of the right aisle, about four meters from the ground.

name: Pont du Christ
description: This bridge was the scene of fierce fighting between the fusilier battalion of the 1st Kurmark Landwehr Regiment and the Swiss soldiers of the STOFFEL battalion, of the 2nd Foreign Regiment, integrated into the 10th Division of the 3rd Corps of the French Army.

name: Battlefield 1st Action of Genappe
description: On June 17th 1815, Uxbridge halted the Heavy Brigades and posted them so as to cover the retirement of the light cavalry. He formed them in line; Somerset's on the right, and Ponsonby's on the left about 660 yards from the river.

name: Battlefield 2nd Action of Genappe
description: On June 18th 1815, it was here that Marshal Lobau collected three hundred men together and tried to make a stand to cover the French Army retreat.

name: Battlefield Action of Namur
description: On June 20th 1815, The Prussian columns of attack, advancing at the pas de charge (at the double), drove the French out of the Namur suburb, and endeavoured to gain possession of the gates of the town.

name: Battlefield of Thouars
description: This battle of Thouars took place on June 20th 1815 during the War in the Vendée of 1815; it was won by the army of the First Empire.

name: Battlefield of Villers-Coterêts
description: Prussian surprise night attack by General Pirch II on June 28th 1815.

name: Sépulture du sous-lieutenant Marie
description: Second Lieutenant Marie Étienne Maximin died on June 28th 1815 in the Retz forest.

name: Battlefield of Ölper
description: Took place on August 1st 1809 in Ölper (currently a district of the town of Brunswick). It pitched troops of the Kingdom of Westphalia against the Black Brunswickers under Frederick William, Duke of Brunswick-Wolfenbüttel.

name: Ölper Battle Memorial
description: The obelisk is 22 meters high and made of ore. It served the memory of the two fallen dukes Carl Wilhelm Ferdinand and Friedrich Wilhelm.

name: Suvorov Monument
description: Erected in 1898, commemorates the battles of 1799 between the Napoleonic troops under Lecourbe and the Russians under Suvorov.

name: Battlefield of Vitebsk
description: Took place on July 26th and 27th 1812 and ended with the Russian forces making a strategic retreat from the battlefield.

name: Battlefield of Ostrovno
description: It took place on July 25th 1812, between French forces under the command of King of Naples Joachim Murat and Russian forces under General Ostermann-Tolstoy and ended with the Russian forces retreating from the battlefield.

name: Battlefield of Shevardino
description: Took place on September 5th [O.S. August 24th] 1812 between French and Russian troops, with a victory of the French army.

name: Battlefield of Krasnoi
description: It unfolded from November 15th to the 18th 1812 marking a critical episode in Napoleon's arduous retreat from Moscow.

name: Battlefield of Vyazma
description: occurred at the beginning of Napoleon's retreat from Moscow on November 3rd 1812 (22 October by OS).

name: Battlefield of Gorodechno
description: It took place on August 12th 1812 between an army of the Austrian and Saxon allies of Napoleonic France under the overall command of the Prince of Schwarzenberg and Russian troops under Alexander Tormasov at Gorodechno.

name: Battlefield of Valutino (Lubino)
description: Also called the battle of Lubino, took place on August 19th 1812 near Valutino and Lubino, between a corps of French and allied troops led by Marshal Ney, about 35,000 strong, and a strong rear-guard of General Barclay de Tolly's Russian army of about 25,000.

name: Battlefield of Dahlenkirchen
description: took place on August 21st 1812, between French and allied troops, with a victory of the Russian army of about 6,000 against the French about 1,500 strong.

name: Battlefield of Mesothen (Mesoten)
description: Took place from September 26th to October 1st 1812, between the Russian Corps of Finland and the French-allied Prussian Auxiliary Corps.

name: Battlefield of Tarutino
description: Russian troops under the general command of Bennigsen attacked and defeated French troops under Murat's command on October 18th 1812.

name: Battlefield of Chashniki
description: Fought on October 31st 1812, between Russian forces under General Wittgenstein, and the French Army, commanded by Marshal Victor.

name: Battle of La Rothière
description: February 1st 1814 saw the Coalition forces of the Austrian Empire, Russian Empire, Kingdom of Prussia, Kingdom of Bavaria, and Kingdom of Württemberg attack a French army led by Emperor Napoleon.

name: Battlefield combat of Lesmont
description: A Coalition force of Russians and Bavarians under generals Eugen of Wurtemberg and Carl Philipp von Wrede was defeated on February 2nd 1814 by a French force under general Joseph Lagrange.

name: Battlefield of Champaubert
description: February 10th 1814 was the opening engagement of the Six Days' Campaign. It was fought between a French army led by Emperor Napoleon and a small Russian corps commanded by Lieutenant General Count Zakhar Dmitrievich Olsufiev.

name: Battlefield of Vauchamps
description: February 14th 1814, was the final major engagement of the Six Days Campaign. It resulted in a part of the Grande Armée under Napoleon I defeating a superior Prussian and Russian force.

name: Colonne commémorative de la bataille de Champaubert
description: Column erected in 1865-1867 in Euville stone based on plans by architects Louis Visconti (author of the Emperor's tomb at Les Invalides).

name: Monument Battle of Raab
description: erected in 1897 on the site where, on the morning of the Battle of Raab (June 14th, 1809), the infantry of Prince Eugene's Army of Italy had deployed.(Photo: Judit Juhász)

name: Battlefield of Montmirail
description: It was fought on February 11th 1814 between a French force led by Emperor Napoleon and two Allied corps commanded by Fabian Wilhelm von Osten-Sacken and Ludwig Yorck von Wartenburg.

name: Battlefield of Montereau
description: An Imperial French army led by Emperor Napoleon fought a corps of Austrians and Württembergers commanded by Crown Prince Frederick William of Württemberg.

name: Battlefield of Lippa
description: It was an engagement which took place on September 7th 1813 in what is now Croatia where the Austrians defeated the Franco-Italians.

name: Battlefield of Arnhem
description: Also known as the storming of Arnhem, saw Friedrich Wilhelm Freiherr von Bülow's Prussian corps fight an Imperial French division under Henri François Marie Charpentier on November 30th 1813

name: Battlefield of Mormant
description: Emperor Napoleon I and a division of Russians under Count Peter Petrovich Pahlen clashed near the town of Mormant, some 50 km (31 mi) southeast of Paris on February 17th 1814.

name: Battlefield of Bayonne
description: It was the last major battle of the Peninsular War, ensued when the French garrison of Bayonne led by General of Division Pierre Thouvenot launched a sortie against a besieging force of British, Portuguese, and Spanish troops on April 14th 1814.

name: Battlefield of Nive
description: It was fought towards the end of the Peninsular War from December 9th to December 13th 1813. Arthur Wellesley, Marquess of Wellington's Anglo-Portuguese and Spanish army defeated Marshal Nicolas Soult's French army.

name: Battlefield of Czarnowo
description: At 4pm on December 23rd 1806 French troops under the eye of Emperor Napoleon I launched an evening assault crossing of the Wkra River against Lieutenant General Alexander Ivanovich Ostermann-Tolstoy's defending Russian Empire forces.

name: Battlefield of Laon
description: On March 9th 1814 Blücher's Prusso-Russian force, occupying a tough defensive position at the town of Laon, repelled attacks by and successfully counterattacked French Emperor Napoleon and his subordinate corps commander, Marshal Marmont.

name: Finckenstein Palace
description: Napoleon's residence from April through June of 1807.

name: Battlefield of Fère-Champenoise
description: It was fought on march 25th 1814 between two Imperial French corps led by Marshals Auguste de Marmont and Édouard Mortier, duc de Trévise and a larger Coalition force composed of cavalry from the Austrian Empire, Kingdom of Prussia, Kingdom of Württemberg, and Russian Empire.

name: Battlefield of Craonne
description: This battle between an Imperial French army under Emperor Napoleon I opposing a combined army of Imperial Russians and Prussians led by Prussian Field Marshal Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher fought on March 7th 1814

name: Battlefield of 1st Bar-sur-Aube
description: Also named Colombey-les-Deux-Églises. It took place on January 24, 1814. It began when the French Imperial Guard corps of the Duke of Trévise, Marshal Édouard Mortier, defended itself against an Austrian corps commanded by Ignácz Gyulay and a Württemberg corps.

name: Battlefield of 2nd Bar-sur-Aube
description: Fought on February 27th 1814, between the First French Empire and the Austrian Empire. French forces were led by Jacques MacDonald, while the Austrians and their Bavarian allies, forming the Army of Bohemia, were led by Karl Philipp Fürst zu Schwarzenberg.

name: Bavarian Army Museum Ingolstadt
description: Part of the exhibition 'Forms of War 1600-1815' includes items from the Napoleonic Wars.

name: Battlefield of Nivelle
description: It took place in front of the river Nivelle with a 30 km front (20 miles) near the end of the Peninsular War.

name: Battlefield of Tolentino
description: was fought from May 2nd to May 3rd 1815 and it was the decisive battle in the Neapolitan War.

name: Rancia Castle- Battle of Tolentino
description: The castle was hotly contested during the Battle of Tolentino.
